error.signature: AWS Lambda function times out or takes 10s+ on first invocation when in VPC

verdict.summary: Lambda functions in a VPC require ENI (Elastic Network Interface) creation on cold start. Before Hyperplane (2019+), this added 10-30s. Post-Hyperplane it's ~1-2s but still catches people. Provisioned concurrency is the real fix.

dead_ends.action: Increase Lambda timeout to 5 minutes to handle cold start
dead_ends.why_fails: Longer timeout masks the problem. Users still experience 10s+ delays on every cold start.

dead_ends.action: Use a CloudWatch scheduled event to keep Lambda warm
dead_ends.why_fails: Warming pings only keep 1 concurrent instance warm. Second concurrent request still cold starts.

dead_ends.action: Remove VPC configuration to eliminate cold start
dead_ends.why_fails: Function loses access to VPC resources (RDS, ElastiCache, etc.). Defeats the purpose of VPC placement.

workarounds.action: Use Provisioned Concurrency to eliminate cold starts
Use Lambda SnapStart (Java) or keep dependencies minimal to reduce init time
workarounds.how: Reduce deployment package size. Use Lambda layers for shared deps. Lazy-load heavy SDKs.

workarounds.action: Use VPC endpoints (PrivateLink) instead of NAT Gateway for AWS service access
workarounds.how: This lets Lambda access S3/DynamoDB/etc without full VPC setup, reducing cold start penalty
